rationale: The query's core transferable method involves modeling networks (food webs or production networks) via static generative rules, dynamical stability analysis of coupled systems, or endogenous assembly/selection processes. The candidate, however, employs agent-based epidemiological modeling of disease transmission, focusing on intervention strategies and calibration to real-world data—entirely different machinery. The candidate's object (COVID-19 pandemic spread in Australia) is genuinely different from the query's food webs, but the methods do not cohere.
